subject: Statistics
title: Time Series Regression
description: A short lesson to help you apply Time Series Regression.

hook.text: Stock prices, weather, and sales all change over time. Standard regression ignores the order of observations.

buildup.text: Time series regression accounts for trends, seasons, and the fact that each observation depends on its past.

discovery.text: Autocorrelation means today's value is linked to yesterday's. Standard regression assumes independence — and breaks.

twist.text: Two unrelated time series that both trend upward will look correlated. The trend creates a false connection.

climax.text: Proper time series methods remove trends and seasons first, then model what's left.

punchline.text: Order matters. Time series regression respects the clock.

quiz.question: Why can't standard regression be used directly on time series data?
options: It assumes independence between observations, but time series data is autocorrelated
It only works with categorical outcomes
